title: A cortical neuron, firing
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Inject a steady current and this two-equation neuron settles into a regular tonic rhythm — spike, reset, recover, repeat. Izhikevich's 2003 model is famous because those four numbers (a, b, c, d) are a dial: nudge them and the same equations become a fast-spiking interneuron, a chattering cell, or an intrinsic burster. Here it's a classic regular-spiking pyramidal cell. The trace is the membrane potential; each vertical stroke is an action potential clipped at its +30 mV peak.
